title: Archives of Evangelion DVD Box and Evangelion Blu ray Box Adding Subtitles Assistance
display_title: Archives of Evangelion DVD Box and Evangelion Blu ray Box Adding Subtitles Assistance
wikitext:
[]: Archives of Evangelion DVD Box and Evangelion Blu-ray Box / Adding Subtitles Assistance             Quote Post by Fifth_Children » Wed Sep 01, 2021 8:31 am So I recently purchased both sets, I was wondering if anyone has any experience or can point me in the right direction on how or what is the best software to rip the discs and add subtitles to them? Where are good subtitle files located? If its possible to burn these ISO's back to disc with English subs added? I know some of this can be done but I've never done it myself and do not know where to start, any assistance would be greatly appreciated!

[]: Re: Archives of Evangelion DVD Box and Evangelion Blu-ray Box / Adding Subtitles Assistance             Quote Post by neongenesisvandelay » Sun Sep 05, 2021 11:55 am I know you can’t edit the Blu-rei’s directly because they’re closed media, but you might have some luck using an external app like Daemon Tools to get the ISO files. You’d probably want to just convert the ISOs into MKV files next then add custom subs to them. Did a quick Google search and came up with this thread, maybe it’ll be of some use: https://forum.videohelp.com/threads/394 ... -a-blu-ray

[]: Re: Archives of Evangelion DVD Box and Evangelion Blu-ray Box / Adding Subtitles Assistance             Quote Post by judyhopps » Sun Mar 09, 2025 11:03 am Hey, were you able to do this? I have the .iso's but would also like to add subtitles so I can burn them

[]: Archives of Evangelion DVD Box and Evangelion Blu-ray Box / Adding Subtitles Assistance             Quote Post by Fifth_Children » Wed Sep 01, 2021 8:31 am So I recently purchased both sets, I was wondering if anyone has any experience or can point me in the right direction on how or what is the best software to rip the discs and add subtitles to them? Where are good subtitle files located? If its possible to burn these ISO's back to disc with English subs added? I know some of this can be done but I've never done it myself and do not know where to start, any assistance would be greatly appreciated!

[]: Re: Archives of Evangelion DVD Box and Evangelion Blu-ray Box / Adding Subtitles Assistance             Quote Post by neongenesisvandelay » Sun Sep 05, 2021 11:55 am I know you can’t edit the Blu-rei’s directly because they’re closed media, but you might have some luck using an external app like Daemon Tools to get the ISO files. You’d probably want to just convert the ISOs into MKV files next then add custom subs to them. Did a quick Google search and came up with this thread, maybe it’ll be of some use: https://forum.videohelp.com/threads/394 ... -a-blu-ray
